description_long: This is a narrow pedestrian suspension bridge constructed primarily from wooden planks, forming a slightly curved deck. The bridge features a simple rope railing system, with vertical posts supporting two parallel ropes on each side for safety. The planks are arranged lengthwise, creating a rustic and utilitarian appearance suitable for parks or natural urban crossings. The structure is lightweight and appears to be designed for foot traffic rather than vehicles. The wood is weathered, suggesting outdoor use, and the rope railings are functional rather than decorative. The overall design is minimalistic, prioritizing function and ease of assembly over ornate details.
description: A narrow wooden suspension bridge with rope railings and a slightly curved deck, designed for pedestrian use. The structure is rustic and utilitarian, with weathered wood planks and simple rope supports.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length and gentle curve of the wooden deck, with rope railings and vertical posts visible.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the narrow width and the alignment of the wooden planks, with the rope railings framing the walkway.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the length, curve, and rope railing system from the other side.
description_view_3: End-on view from the opposite end, again emphasizing the narrow width and the straight alignment of the planks.
attribute_railing_design: rope railings with vertical wooden posts, simple and functional
